claims.claim: Paper title is 'Scalable and Training-Free Ranking from Pairwise Comparisons via Acyclic Graph Construction'

manuscript_values.title: Scalable and Training-Free Ranking from Pairwise Comparisons via Acyclic Graph Construction

claims.notes: No paper PDF or title declaration found in repository artifacts

claims.claim: Method is scalable, deterministic, training-free pipeline based on MFAS viewpoint

repo_values.description: designed to be fast, deterministic, and time-limit friendly; no learnable parameters

claims.notes: Source code header and implementation confirm all three properties

claims.claim: Method family includes OURS-MFAS, OURS-MFAS-INS1, OURS-MFAS-INS2, OURS-MFAS-INS3
claims.notes: All four methods confirmed in leaderboard CSVs

claims.claim: Pipeline: Phase 1 local-ratio-style cycle breaking, Phase 2 stable weight-prioritized add-back with up to 3 passes, optional score refinement

phases: Phase A: local-ratio MFAS cycle breaking
Phase B: add-back in descending weight order with INS passes (1/2/3)
Phase 2 stable weight-prioritized add-back with up to 3 passes

claims.notes: Code structure in ours_mfas.py exactly matches claimed pipeline

claims.claim: INS1/INS2/INS3 correspond to 1/2/3 add-back passes
claims.notes: Function signature and call sites confirm pass count mapping

claims.claim: Benchmark suite has 80 weighted directed comparison-graph instances
claims.notes: Repo has 81 unique datasets in leaderboard CSVs. The _AUTO/Basketball_temporal__1985adj is an automatically generated variant not in the manuscript's 80-dataset description. This causes systematic +1 discrepancy in all coverage denominators.

claims.claim: Composition: 60 basketball, 12 football, 3 faculty hiring, 1 animal, 2 head-to-head, 1 finance, 1 ERO-style
claims.notes: Composition totals to 80 and matches manuscript if _AUTO dataset excluded. Two head-to-head datasets are Halo2BetaData and Halo2BetaData/HeadToHead, both n=602 m=5010.

claims.claim: Vertex counts range n=20 to n=1315
claims.notes: All English Premier League datasets have n=20; finance has n=1315

claims.claim: Edge counts range m=107 to about 1.73e6
claims.notes: Exact values: England_2014_2015 m=107, finance m=1,729,225

claims.claim: Basketball 60 n=282-351 m=2904-7650; Football 12 n range 20-107; Faculty Business n=113 m=1787; CS n=206 m=1407; History n=145 m=1204; Animal n=21 m=193; Head-to-head 2 instances n=602 m=5010; Finance n=1315 m=1729225
claims.notes: CONTRADICTED for football: manuscript claims n range 20-107 but ALL 12 football datasets (English Premier League) have exactly n=20. All other sub-claims verified.

claims.claim: Classical baselines: SpringRank, SyncRank, SerialRank, BTL, DavidScore, EigenvectorCentrality, PageRank, RankCentrality, SVD-RS, SVD-NRS
claims.notes: All 10 methods confirmed in source code method list

claims.claim: GNN baselines include DIGRAC and ib backbones
claims.notes: Both backbones appear in result arrays and all leaderboard CSVs

claims.claim: For each GNN backbone there are two fixed benchmark configurations
claims.notes: Two config templates confirmed; multiple K values exist but the two main architectural configs are dist and proximal_baseline

claims.claim: Non-GNN methods: timeout 1800s
claims.notes: Source code line 37: DEFAULT_METHOD_TIMEOUT = 1800

claims.claim: GNN methods: timeout 7200s
claims.notes: Source code line 38: GNN_METHOD_TIMEOUT = 7200

claims.claim: TRIALS=10 repeated runs
claims.notes: run_full.sh explicitly sets TRIALS=10

claims.claim: Real datasets use fixed internal seed(s) = [10]
claims.notes: run_full.sh passes --seeds 10 for all real datasets; param_parser sets args.seeds=[10] for real data

claims.claim: Timed-out runs produce NaN, excluded via NaN-robust averaging

repo_values.mechanism: signal.SIGALRM timeout handler stores NaN; aggregation uses dropna()

claims.notes: Mechanism confirmed in source code

claims.claim: There is a compute-matched view under shared 1800s budget
claims.notes: table2_compute_matched.csv exists and is populated

claims.claim: Compute-matched dataset set has 79 datasets (ERO-style doesn't complete within 1800s)
claims.notes: Coverage denominators in table2_compute_matched.csv show /80 not /79. Same systematic +1 discrepancy as B6.

claims.claim: OURS-MFAS Table 4: median_simple=0.878049 mean=0.887880; median_ratio=0.505748 mean=0.381891; median_naive=0.219512 mean=0.221970; coverage 77/80
claims.notes: All metric values match to 6 significant figures. Coverage numerator (77) matches; denominator is 81 in repo vs 80 in manuscript due to _AUTO dataset.

claims.claim: OURS-MFAS-INS3 Table 4: same as C21; coverage 77/80
claims.notes: INS3 identical to OURS_MFAS on all 77 datasets (confirmed). Coverage denominator off by 1.

claims.claim: OURS-MFAS-INS2 Table 4: median_simple=0.878049 mean=0.887914; median_ratio=0.505748 mean=0.381874; median_naive=0.219512 mean=0.221979; coverage 77/80
claims.notes: All values verified; INS2 differs from INS3 only in mean values (3rd decimal), as expected.

claims.claim: OURS-MFAS-INS1 Table 4: median_simple=0.880927 mean=0.888633; median_ratio=0.502815 mean=0.381915; median_naive=0.220232 mean=0.222158; coverage 77/80
claims.notes: All metric values match to 6 significant figures. Coverage denominator off by 1.

claims.claim: BTL Table 4: median_simple=0.825000 mean=0.835385; median_ratio=0.631922 mean=0.575385; median_naive=0.206250 mean=0.208718; coverage 78/80
claims.notes: MAJOR DISCREPANCY. Manuscript BTL mean_simple=0.835385 matches repo davidScore mean_simple=0.835393 to 5 decimal places — possible method-label swap between BTL and DavidScore.

claims.claim: RankCentrality Table 4: median_simple=1.000000 mean=1.010641; median_ratio=0.646415 mean=0.573846; median_naive=0.250000 mean=0.252949; coverage 78/80
claims.notes: Repo value ~2x manuscript value for median_simple

claims.claim: SerialRank Table 4: median_simple=1.075000 mean=1.093333; median_ratio=0.307267 mean=0.296795; median_naive=0.268750 mean=0.273333; coverage 78/80
claims.notes: Manuscript SerialRank median=1.075 ≈ repo PageRank median=1.075982 — possible label confusion

claims.claim: SpringRank Table 4: median_simple=1.675000 mean=1.679615; median_ratio=0.476411 mean=0.491154; median_naive=0.418750 mean=0.420256; coverage 78/80
claims.notes: Directions reversed: repo shows SpringRank BETTER than OURS (0.802 < 0.878), manuscript shows it WORSE (1.675 > 0.878)

claims.claim: SyncRank Table 4: median_simple=1.825000 mean=1.833462; median_ratio=0.476411 mean=0.490641; median_naive=0.456250 mean=0.461795; coverage 78/80
claims.notes: Roughly same order of magnitude but values differ by ~6%; coverage numerator also differs (77 vs 78)

claims.claim: PageRank Table 4: median_simple=1.925000 mean=1.934615; median_ratio=0.960686 mean=0.973974; median_naive=0.481250 mean=0.488846; coverage 78/80
claims.notes: Manuscript PageRank values match repo serialRank more closely

claims.claim: DavidScore Table 4: median_simple=0.925000 mean=0.939103; median_ratio=0.632356 mean=0.578957; median_naive=0.231250 mean=0.234615; coverage 78/80
claims.notes: Repo DavidScore mean=0.835393 ≈ manuscript BTL mean=0.835385 — same swap pattern as C25

claims.claim: EigenvectorCentrality Table 4: median_simple=1.825000 mean=1.833077; median_ratio=0.960686 mean=0.973623; median_naive=0.456250 mean=0.461603; coverage 78/80
claims.notes: Off by ~0.82 for median_simple

claims.claim: SVD-RS Table 4: median_simple=0.880927 mean=0.890513; median_ratio=0.790599 mean=0.550128; median_naive=0.220232 mean=0.222949; coverage 78/80
claims.notes: Manuscript SVD-RS median=0.880927 is the exact same value as OURS_MFAS_INS1 — possible copy-paste error

claims.claim: SVD-NRS Table 4: median_simple=0.882925 mean=0.898333; median_ratio=0.489421 mean=0.380128; median_naive=0.220732 mean=0.224744; coverage 78/80
claims.notes: Closest match among classical methods (~1% off for simple) but ratio values differ substantially

claims.claim: OURS family: datasets with metrics 77, runtime 77, any timeout 1
claims.notes: Claim matches OURS_MFAS specifically. The INS variants each have 80 datasets with valid metrics (not 77). Family aggregate is 317 total, not 77.

claims.claim: Classical family: datasets with metrics 79, runtime 79, any timeout 2
claims.notes: No individual classical method or family aggregate matches the claimed 79/79/2 values

claims.claim: GNN family: datasets with metrics 78, runtime 78, any timeout 2
claims.notes: ib has 2 timeouts matching claim, but metrics count (157 each) doesn't match 78

claims.claim: Other family: datasets with metrics 1, runtime 0, any timeout 1
claims.notes: btlDIGRAC (other family) exactly matches: 1 metric, 0 runtime, 1 timeout

claims.claim: OURS means OURS-MFAS-INS3 in headline summaries
claims.notes: compute_wtl_by_metric.py docstring: 'OURS method: OURS_MFAS_INS3'

claims.claim: OURS vs classical: W/T/L = 38/0/39 upset-simple; 45/0/32 upset-ratio; within 10% = 42/77 simple, 47/77 ratio
claims.notes: Exact match across all W/T/L and within-10% statistics

claims.claim: OURS vs GNN: W/T/L = 45/1/31 upset-simple; 47/0/30 upset-ratio; within 10% = 48/77, 47/77
claims.notes: Exact match

claims.claim: Ties defined as |delta| <= 1e-6

repo_values.tie_threshold: 1e-6 (W_1e6 columns identical to W_1e3 columns for this dataset)

claims.notes: contribution_stats.csv has both _1e6 and _1e3 suffixed columns; manuscript uses 1e-6

claims.notes: Exact match

claims.notes: Formula confirmed in contribution_report.md

claims.notes: Values match to 4 significant figures

claims.claim: Mean speedup = 62.69x
claims.notes: Exact match to 4 significant figures

claims.claim: Datasets with speedup >= 10x / 50x / 100x = 38 / 17 / 13

claims.claim: Finance times out for all OURS variants under 1800s budget
claims.notes: Only OURS_MFAS times out on finance. INS1/2/3 complete within 1800s (finance:included). Claim is only partially correct.

claims.claim: Accuracy tail concentrated in finer basketball instances
claims.notes: All 20 worst-case datasets by rel_gap_simple vs best GNN are Basketball_temporal/finer* instances

claims.claim: OURS never slower than GNN
claims.notes: 0 datasets where OURS is slower in either better or worse outcome

claims.claim: Comparisons only on datasets with valid outputs

claims.notes: Source code confirms NaN exclusion in comparisons

claims.notes: No specific rerun log or result file found documenting this claim

required_fixes.description: Benchmark count should be 81 (repo) not 80 (manuscript), or the _AUTO/Basketball_temporal__1985adj dataset should be explicitly excluded from the benchmark definition and all CSVs updated.
Compute-matched suite has 80 datasets (repo), not 79. Update manuscript Table 5 denominator to 80 or exclude _AUTO from all analyses.

All classical method Table 4 values are contradicted by repo. Regenerate from leaderboard_per_method.csv (trials10). Correct values: SpringRank median_simple=0.802724; BTL=0.984385; DavidScore=0.824138; SyncRank=1.716463; RankCentrality=1.944012; SerialRank=1.951618; PageRank=1.075982; EigenvectorCentrality=1.006061; SVD_RS=0.987070; SVD_NRS=0.891564. Note potential label swap between BTL and DavidScore.
Classical method Table 5 (compute-matched) values inherit the same error from Table 4. Regenerate from table2_compute_matched.csv.
Football 12 instances n range should be 20-20 (all n=20), not 20-107. All English Premier League datasets have exactly 20 teams.
Coverage denominators should be /81 (Table 4) and /80 (Table 5). Manuscript says /80 and /79 respectively. Resolve by either excluding _AUTO dataset from analyses or updating manuscript.
Classical family (E49) and GNN family (E50) missingness claim values do not match repo aggregates. Clarify whether claims are about individual methods or family totals.
Finance times out only for OURS_MFAS, not for OURS_MFAS_INS1/2/3. Qualify the claim accordingly.
The rerun claim for two tight-margin football instances is uncheckable. Either cite a specific log file or remove the claim.
